clubs.name: Parkdale Roadrunners
clubs.social_signal: Strong social vibe — known for post-run brewery/cafe meetups in Parkdale, one of the most explicitly social-coded clubs in Toronto. Pints + cafe culture baked in.
clubs.best_for: social entry + fast intervals + making friends 25-35

clubs.name: BlackToe Running - King West (Tuesday)
clubs.social_signal: Coached/structured — show-up-and-grind on Tue. Friday tempo + Sun long runs are more casual. Some post-run socializing at the store but less pub-coded than Parkdale.
clubs.best_for: fast 100km/wk runner wanting coached workouts

clubs.name: BlackToe Running - Drop-in (Fri/Sun)
clubs.social_signal: Sun long run is the most social — distances 12-24km, varied pace groups, store culture. unknown — verify in person whether pub follows.
clubs.best_for: weekend long-run partners at 100km/wk volume

clubs.name: BlackToe Running - Midtown (Wednesday)
clubs.social_signal: Coached structured workout — similar grind feel to King West Tue. unknown — verify post-run social in person
clubs.best_for: fast structured workouts

clubs.name: Tribe Fitness
clubs.meet_days: Wed 5km fun run + Sat long #bRUNch run
meet_locations: Canoe Landing Park area / 10 Lower Spadina vicinity — verify current HQ
clubs.social_signal: Explicit social — '#bRUNch' brand. Brunch after Sat run. Less of a workout, more of a social jog. Strong community/making-friends vibe.
clubs.best_for: social entry / making friends — but pace may be slow for 4:30-5:30/km runner

clubs.name: Longboat Roadrunners
clubs.meet_days: Wed evening + Sun morning long run (Fri seasonal)
clubs.social_signal: Monthly pub night first Wednesday of every month — established social tradition. Founded 1980, so culture is real but demographic skews older than Parkdale/Tribe.
clubs.best_for: weekend long-run partners, structured competitive club

clubs.name: Toronto Running Club (TRC)
clubs.social_signal: Established since 1995, social but more middle-of-the-road. unknown — verify pub culture in person.
clubs.best_for: consistent structured runs, midtown access

clubs.name: High Park Rogue Runners
clubs.meet_days: Wed 6:30pm + Fri 6:30pm workouts + Sun 8:30am long run
meet_locations: North High Park gates (Bloor St & High Park Ave) — 'The Nub'
clubs.social_signal: Pace-grouped Sun long runs (5:00-7:00/km tiers). Volunteer-led, performance-oriented, community-supportive (donates to charities). Less explicit pub culture — more grind-and-go. unknown — verify post-run social in person.
clubs.best_for: fast 100km/wk runner — closest to Mississauga geographically, fast pace groups available

clubs.name: Humber Bay Run Club (@hbsrunclub)
meet_locations: Bike Share Toronto, Humber Bay Shores Park / Marine Parade Dr, Etobicoke
clubs.social_signal: South Etobicoke 'friendly neighbourhood' club. Has done collab events (e.g., GLB Run Club crossovers). Likely moderate social vibe — neighborhood-coded not party-coded. unknown — verify in person.
clubs.best_for: GEOGRAPHIC FIT — closest social club to Credit Valley Hospital. Easy commute from Mississauga.

clubs.name: Kingsway Run Club
clubs.social_signal: unknown — verify in person. Small neighborhood club.
clubs.best_for: Etobicoke geographic fit, commute-friendly from Mississauga

clubs.name: Toronto West Athletics (TWA)
meet_locations: Toronto Track & Field Center, York University (Jan-Mar indoor)
clubs.social_signal: Coached, performance-oriented. Show-up-and-train, not pub-coded. Strong fit for the fast/serious training crowd.
clubs.best_for: fast 100km/wk runner wanting coached west-side training — Centennial Park is ~15 min from Credit Valley Hospital

clubs.name: Port Credit Road Runners
clubs.social_signal: Friendly small-club vibe, all levels, distance-running focused. Older demographic than downtown Toronto clubs. unknown — verify post-run social culture.
clubs.best_for: Mississauga local convenience, geographically closest to CVH

clubs.name: Nomads Running Club Mississauga
clubs.social_signal: Founded 2004, 'masters' social group. Friendship-focused, mid-distance 5-10km. Older crowd than user's target. Friendly but not 25-35 social scene.
clubs.best_for: Mississauga proximity, but demographic mismatch

clubs.name: Meadowvale Panthers / Meadowvale parkrun
meet_locations: Lake Aquitaine Park, 6655 Glen Erin Dr, Mississauga
clubs.social_signal: parkrun = family/community vibe, post-run coffee is global parkrun tradition. Not a singles scene. 'No runner left behind' inclusive culture.
clubs.best_for: casual Saturday social, Mississauga local, but mismatch for 25-35 dating-scene angle

clubs.name: Running Room - Square One (Mississauga)
meet_locations: 55 Square One Drive, Mississauga (status uncertain — Yelp lists as CLOSED; verify Argentia Rd 2969 location)
clubs.social_signal: Chain-store clinic — structured, beginner-marathon-prep oriented. Not a singles social scene. Pace likely too slow for 4:30-5:30/km user.
clubs.best_for: marathon clinic structure if needed, otherwise low fit

clubs.name: Running Room - Winston Churchill (Mississauga)
clubs.social_signal: Same as Square One — clinic structure, older skew. Mississauga-local.
clubs.best_for: Mississauga proximity for marathon prep clinics

clubs.name: Mississauga Track & Field Club
clubs.social_signal: Volunteer not-for-profit, since 1969. Competitive track focus, not social scene. Useful for speed work.
clubs.best_for: fast 100km/wk runner needing track sessions in Mississauga

clubs.name: WildStride - Etobicoke Trail Runners
clubs.social_signal: Meetup.com group — meetup culture is more open/social-by-design, but trail-running crowd tends to be lower-key than urban running crews. unknown — verify.
clubs.best_for: trail running variety, geographic fit for west Etobicoke / CVH commuter

clubs.name: MiBody Runners
clubs.social_signal: Studio-attached run group — fitness/wellness vibe. 5K/8K/10K options. Likely social but small. unknown — verify in person.
clubs.best_for: Etobicoke waterfront social, small group setting

clubs.name: Midnight Runners Toronto
clubs.social_signal: EXPLICITLY social/party-coded — bootcamp runs with music, party runs (Halloween, mid-summer) with afterparties. 10K+ Toronto members on Strava. One of the most singles-scene-coded clubs.
clubs.best_for: social entry / making friends 25-35 / explicit party-after culture

clubs.name: Curre Club
clubs.social_signal: Early-morning social — coffee built-in (meets at Neo Coffee). Front group at 6:00/km. Co-founder-led, friendly. Distinct early-bird vibe.
clubs.best_for: social downtown morning crowd, low-commitment Friday

clubs.name: Portland Runners Toronto
clubs.social_signal: Ace Hotel as meeting point implies design/young-pro crowd. Smaller intimate group. unknown — verify post-run drinks at Ace bar.
clubs.best_for: smaller social group, design-forward crowd, Ace Hotel proximity

clubs.name: Beaches Runners Club (BRC)
clubs.social_signal: Founded 2010, one of the largest social clubs in Toronto. Free membership. East-end community feel. Geographically far from Mississauga.
clubs.best_for: social east-end crowd but commute from CVH/Mississauga is brutal (45+ min)

clubs.name: Run the Beaches TO
clubs.social_signal: unknown — smaller social group, evening waterfront runs.

clubs.name: Running Rats Run Club
clubs.social_signal: Founded 2002, requires 7km baseline fitness. Established community. unknown — verify pub culture.

meta.top_5_rationale: User is a 27yo ER physician based at Credit Valley Hospital (Mississauga), runs 100km/wk at 4:30-5:30/km, and wants social entry. Geographic + pace + social vibe filter: (1) Humber Bay = closest social club to CVH, dense Etobicoke meets 4x/wk; (2) Parkdale Roadrunners = highest social/singles density in Toronto, on the QEW corridor from Mississauga, Tue Stay Gold + Sat Bather + explicit pub culture; (3) High Park Rogue Runners = west-side, fast pace groups, Sun long-run partner pool, free; (4) TWA = Centennial Park is ~15min from CVH, coached fast training fits 100km/wk volume; (5) Midnight Runners = most explicit party/social-scene-coded Toronto crew, downtown though so commute is a factor.
anti_picks: Nomads Mississauga / Running Room clinics — demographics skew 35-55, marathon-clinic crowd not 25-35 social scene
Beaches BRC + Eastbound — east-end commute from Mississauga is 45+ min, kills viability
lululemon clubs — pace 5:30-6:30/km too slow for user's training intensity
meta.notes: Most explicitly-singles-coded run clubs are in Toronto (Parkdale, Midnight Runners, Portland Runners, Beer Run, Curre Club). Mississauga clubs (Nomads, Port Credit, parkrun) skew older and lack the after-run-pub culture. The geographic compromise for a CVH-based attending is South Etobicoke / Humber Bay / Parkdale (QEW corridor, 20-30 min commute) — and that's exactly where the social-coded clubs cluster. HBS Run Club is the single best 'live in west Mississauga / Etobicoke, get social density' anchor. For weekend long runs at pace, HPRR Sunday is the standard. Clubs the user mentioned that were NOT confirmed in searches: Athletic Avenue, Newstalgia, BLKHRT, Sole Sisters (Toronto), Sundays Run Club, Run TO, Roncesvalles Runners, Westside Runners — recommend verifying these names with whoever referenced them (may be IG-only micro-crews, misspellings, or generic descriptions, not formal clubs). All paces are estimates based on directory listings; verify with each club's pace groups in person.
